Rule description:
Found '$SPAWN' with '{shell: process.platform === "win32"}'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use '{shell: false}' instead.

Autotriage reason:
This matched because the rule treats any non-false shell value as dangerous, but here the shell option is only enabled on Windows for a fixed hardcoded command and arguments. There is no user-controlled input flowing into the command, so this is not a meaningful command injection risk in this context.
